title: Taeniophyllum carinatum
taxonomy: Taeniophyllum carinatum is classified as a species of orchid. Specifically, it belongs to the genus Taeniophyllum, which is a group of orchids found primarily in Asia. The genus Taeniophyllum is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae, within the family Orchidaceae. The subfamily Orchidoideae is a large and diverse group of orchids that includes many species found in tropical and subtropical regions. Taeniophyllum carinatum is further classified as a flowering plant, with the kingdom and phylum being Plantae and Magnoliophyta, respectively.
uses: Taeniophyllum carinatum is known to have various medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used in Southeast Asian countries to treat fever, respiratory issues, and gastrointestinal problems.
